tluaNoArray.tlua(25,6): error TLUA1109: Expression expected.
tluaNoArray.tlua(25,14): error TLUA1005: ';' expected.
tluaNoArray.tlua(25,14): error TLUA2304: Cannot find name 'of'.
tluaNoArray.tlua(25,17): error TLUA1434: Unexpected keyword or identifier.
tluaNoArray.tlua(25,22): error TLUA1128: Declaration or statement expected.
tluaNoArray.tlua(31,7): error TLUA2322: Type 'number' is not assignable to type '[number, string]'.
tluaNoArray.tlua(36,1): error TLUA1005: 'end' expected.


==== tluaNoArray.tlua (27 errors) ====
    // The TypeScript array-literal/iteration syntax is removed from tlua source.
    // Each form below decomposes into stock parser-recovery errors (no
    // tlua-specific diagnostic), exactly like the class/generator removals.
    // (Tuple *type* syntax `[A, B]` is legal — see the tluaTuple tests.)
    
    declare function pair(): (number, string);
    declare items: number[];
    declare function use(x: unknown): void;
    
    // Array literals no longer parse in expression position; use a table `{...}`.
    local lit = [1, 2, 3];
                ~
!!! error TLUA1109: Expression expected.
                 ~
!!! error TLUA2695: Left side of comma operator is unused and has no side effects.
                 ~~~~
!!! error TLUA2695: Left side of comma operator is unused and has no side effects.
    
    // Array-literal spread. Every spread form is gone; `...` is the Lua vararg, so
    // the `...` here reads as a vararg expression (legal in the main chunk) and the
    // operand that follows is the error.
    local spread = [0, ...items];
                   ~
!!! error TLUA1109: Expression expected.
                    ~
!!! error TLUA2695: Left side of comma operator is unused and has no side effects.
                          ~~~~~
!!! error TLUA1005: ']' expected.
                               ~
!!! error TLUA1128: Declaration or statement expected.
    
    // Array binding patterns; use a Lua name list `local a, b = f()`.
    local [a, b] = pair();
          ~
!!! error TLUA1003: Identifier expected.
           ~
!!! error TLUA2304: Cannot find name 'a'.
           ~
!!! error TLUA2695: Left side of comma operator is unused and has no side effects.
              ~
!!! error TLUA2304: Cannot find name 'b'.
               ~
!!! error TLUA1005: ';' expected.
                 ~
!!! error TLUA1128: Declaration or statement expected.
    
    // Array destructuring assignment.
    [a, b] = pair();
    ~
!!! error TLUA1128: Declaration or statement expected.
     ~
!!! error TLUA2304: Cannot find name 'a'.
     ~
!!! error TLUA2695: Left side of comma operator is unused and has no side effects.
        ~
!!! error TLUA2304: Cannot find name 'b'.
         ~
!!! error TLUA1005: ';' expected.
           ~
!!! error TLUA1128: Declaration or statement expected.
    
    // `for..of`; use a Lua generic-for `for _, v in ipairs(t) do`.
    for (local v of items) {
        ~
!!! error TLUA1003: Identifier expected.
         ~~~~~
!!! error TLUA1109: Expression expected.
                 ~~
!!! error TLUA1005: ';' expected.
                 ~~
!!! error TLUA2304: Cannot find name 'of'.
                    ~~~~~
!!! error TLUA1434: Unexpected keyword or identifier.
                         ~
!!! error TLUA1128: Declaration or statement expected.
      use(v);
    }
    
    // Tuple types parse in source, and a multireturn is not a tuple value: only
    // the first value survives outside a value-list tail, so this is an error.
    local t: [number, string] = pair();
          ~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type 'number' is not assignable to type '[number, string]'.
    
    // `of` is an ordinary identifier again.
    local of = 3;
    use(of);
    
    
!!! error TLUA1005: 'end' expected.
